title: Electronic Waste Recycling Act of 2003
notes: Advance Recovery Fee model; covers video display devices (TVs, monitors, laptops).

title: Maine Electronic Waste Recycling Law
notes: First state EPR/producer-responsibility model for e-waste; covers TVs, monitors, computers.

title: Maryland Statewide Computer Recycling Pilot Program / Electronics Recycling
notes: Manufacturer registration-fee model; covers computers and TVs.

title: Electronic Product Recycling (E-Cycle Washington)
notes: Producer-responsibility model; covers computers, monitors, TVs, portables.

title: Connecticut Electronics Recycling Law
notes: Producer-responsibility model; covers computers, monitors, TVs, printers.

title: Minnesota Electronics Recycling Act
notes: Manufacturer performance-based recycling targets for video display devices.

title: Oregon E-Cycles
notes: Producer-responsibility model; covers computers, monitors, TVs, printers.

title: Texas Computer Equipment / TV Recycling Programs
notes: Manufacturer takeback for computers (2007) and TVs (2011).

title: North Carolina Electronics Management / Disposal Ban
notes: Producer-responsibility plus landfill disposal ban; covers computers and TVs.

title: New Jersey Electronic Waste Management Act
notes: Producer-responsibility model; covers computers, monitors, TVs, portables.

title: Oklahoma Computer Equipment Recovery Act
notes: Manufacturer takeback for computer equipment.

title: Virginia Computer Recovery and Recycling Act
notes: Manufacturer takeback for computers.

title: West Virginia Covered Electronic Devices Takeback Program
notes: Manufacturer registration and takeback; covers computers, monitors, TVs.

title: Missouri Manufacturer Responsibility and Consumer Convenience Equipment Collection and Recovery Act
notes: Manufacturer takeback for computer equipment.

title: Hawaii Electronic Waste and Television Recycling and Recovery Act
notes: Producer-responsibility model; covers computers, monitors, printers, TVs.

title: Rhode Island Electronic Waste Prevention, Reuse and Recycling Act
notes: Producer-responsibility model; covers computers, monitors, TVs.

title: Illinois Electronic Products Recycling and Reuse Act
notes: Manufacturer recycling targets plus landfill disposal ban.

title: Michigan Electronic Waste Takeback Program
notes: Manufacturer registration and takeback; covers computers and TVs.

title: Indiana Electronic Waste Program
notes: Manufacturer collection-target model; covers video display devices from households/small businesses.

title: Wisconsin E-Cycle Program
notes: Producer-responsibility model plus disposal ban; covers computers, monitors, TVs.

title: Vermont Electronic Waste / E-Cycles Program
notes: Free producer-funded statewide collection; covers computers, monitors, TVs, printers.

title: South Carolina Manufacturer Responsibility and Consumer Convenience IT Equipment Collection and Recovery Act
notes: Manufacturer recycling plus disposal ban; covers computers and TVs.

title: New York State Electronic Equipment Recycling and Reuse Act
notes: Producer-responsibility model with free takeback plus disposal ban.

title: Pennsylvania Covered Device Recycling Act
notes: Manufacturer takeback plus disposal ban; covers computers, monitors, TVs.

title: Utah Electronic Waste Recycling Program
notes: Manufacturer education/reporting-focused model; covers computers, monitors, TVs.

title: District of Columbia Sustainable Solid Waste Management Amendment Act (e-waste provisions)
notes: Producer-responsibility model; covers computers, monitors, printers, TVs.

title: Photovoltaic Module Stewardship and Takeback Program
notes: First U.S. solar EPR law; manufacturer-financed takeback for PV modules (RCW 70A.510); compliance deadline delayed to 2031.

title: Hazardous waste: photovoltaic modules
notes: Authorizes DTSC to manage end-of-life PV modules as universal waste rather than a producer-takeback EPR program.

title: New Jersey Solar Panel Recycling Act
notes: Mandatory solar panel recycling with chain-of-custody tracking; not an EPR/producer-takeback framework.

title: Oregon Architectural Paint Stewardship (PaintCare)
notes: First U.S. paint EPR law (2009); PaintCare program began July 2010 under ORS 459A.820-.855.

title: California Architectural Paint Recovery Program (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began October 2012; codified at Public Resources Code 48700-48706.

title: Connecticut Paint Stewardship Program (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began July 2013.

title: Rhode Island Postconsumer Paint Stewardship (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began June 2014.

title: Vermont Architectural Paint Stewardship (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began May 2014.

title: Minnesota Architectural Paint Stewardship (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began November 2014.

title: Colorado Architectural Paint Stewardship (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began July 2015.

title: Maine Architectural Paint Stewardship (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began October 2015.

title: District of Columbia Paint Stewardship (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began November 2016.

title: Washington Paint Stewardship (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began April 2021.

title: New York Postconsumer Paint Collection (PaintCare)
notes: PaintCare program began May 2022.

title: Illinois Paint Stewardship Act (PaintCare)
notes: Enacted as Public Act 103-372 (SB 836); first program year began Jan 1, 2025.

title: Maryland Paint Stewardship
notes: Enacted as Chapter 588 of 2024; sale ban on non-compliant paint begins Jan 1, 2026.

title: California Carpet Stewardship Program
notes: First U.S. carpet EPR law; administered by CARE under PRC 42968-42983.5.

title: New York Carpet Collection and Recycling (Carpet EPR)
notes: Second U.S. carpet EPR law (S5027C/A9279A); program start postponed to July 1, 2026.

title: California Used Mattress Recovery and Recycling Act
notes: MRC 'Bye Bye Mattress' collection program began 2016.

title: Connecticut Mattress Stewardship Program
notes: First U.S. mattress EPR law; MRC program collecting since 2015.

title: Rhode Island Mattress Stewardship Program
notes: MRC 'Bye Bye Mattress' program began 2016.

title: Oregon Mattress Stewardship Program
notes: Enacted 2022; MRC program began January 1, 2025.

title: Responsible Textile Recovery Act of 2024
notes: First U.S. textile EPR law (Chapter 864); CalRecycle selected Landbell USA as PRO in Feb 2026.

title: Primary Battery Stewardship Law (Act 139)
notes: First-in-nation EPR law requiring single-use/primary battery manufacturers to fund a statewide collection program.

title: Responsible Battery Recycling Act of 2022
notes: Producer-led EPR for all battery chemistries (Chapter 351); supersedes the 2006 Rechargeable Battery Recycling Act.

title: Responsible Environmental Management of Batteries
notes: Broad battery EPR covering most household battery chemistries (Ch. 70A.555 RCW); signed May 11, 2023.

title: Battery and Electronic Stewardship Amendment Act of 2022
notes: Requires producers of primary and rechargeable batteries to join a stewardship org and fund free collection.

title: Portable and Medium-Format Battery Stewardship Act
notes: EPR for portable and medium-format batteries (Public Act 103-1033); signed Aug 9, 2024.

title: Stewardship Program for Primary and Rechargeable Batteries
notes: Listed on Battery Network (Call2Recycle) as an enacted battery stewardship law; specific bill page not separately verified.

title: Rechargeable Battery Management Law
notes: Long-standing rechargeable (Ni-Cd / SSLA) battery take-back law listed by Call2Recycle/Battery Network.

title: Rechargeable Battery Law
notes: Early rechargeable-battery management/labeling law listed by Battery Network.

title: Rechargeable Battery Recycling Law
notes: Statewide rechargeable battery take-back law listed by Battery Network.

title: Rechargeable Battery Management Law
notes: Ni-Cd / sealed lead-acid rechargeable battery management law listed by Battery Network.

title: Rechargeable Battery Management Law
notes: Ni-Cd / sealed lead-acid rechargeable battery management law listed by Battery Network.

title: Rechargeable Battery Management Law
notes: MD Code Environment 6-1107 (Subtitle 11, Rechargeable Batteries); unit management program for Ni-Cd and small sealed lead batteries. Added by Acts 1993, c. 462.

title: Safe Collection and Recycling of Mercury-containing Lighting (38 MRSA 1672)
notes: First-in-nation mercury-lamp EPR requiring manufacturers to fund/operate statewide lamp recycling; PL 2009 c. 272.

title: Mercury-Containing Lights Product Stewardship (LightRecycle)
notes: Producer-funded mercury-lamp stewardship via an environmental handling charge (Ch. 70A.505 RCW).

title: Mercury-Containing Lamp Producer Responsibility Program
notes: EPR program requiring lamp manufacturers to fund/operate statewide mercury-lamp recycling.

title: An Act Relative to Mercury Management
notes: Mercury management act including manufacturer responsibility provisions for mercury-added lamps.

title: Mercury-added Thermostats collection program (38 MRSA 1665-B)
notes: First-in-nation mercury-thermostat EPR requiring manufacturer collection program; PL 2005 c. 558.

title: Mercury Thermostat Collection Act of 2008
notes: Requires manufacturers that sold mercury thermostats to establish collection/recycling programs; administered by DTSC.

title: Mercury-Free Thermostat Act
notes: Bans sale/installation/disposal of mercury thermostats and requires manufacturer collection.

title: Mercury Thermostat Collection Act
notes: Requires thermostat manufacturers to establish collection programs for out-of-service mercury thermostats.

title: Mercury Thermostat Collection requirement
notes: Requires manufacturers to establish collection programs for mercury thermostats.

title: Mercury Thermostat Collection requirement
notes: Requires manufacturers that sold mercury thermostats to participate in a collection program.

title: Mercury Thermostat Collection Act / disposal ban
notes: Disposal ban since 2005 plus manufacturer collection requirements.

title: Mercury Thermostat Collection requirement
notes: Requires thermostat manufacturers to establish collection programs for out-of-service mercury thermostats.

title: Mercury Thermostat Collection / EPR law
notes: Among the states with manufacturer thermostat-collection EPR legislation.

title: Mercury Thermostat Collection / EPR law
notes: Listed among PSI's mercury-thermostat EPR states; TRC maintains a Rhode Island state-law page.

title: Mercury Switch Removal from Motor Vehicles (38 MRSA 1665-A)
notes: Requires removal of mercury switches before crushing and manufacturer collection/compensation; PL 2001 c. 656, administered via ELVS.

title: Mercury Switch Removal Act
notes: Requires auto recyclers to remove mercury switches before crushing; manufacturers pay per switch (ELVS program).

title: Mercury Switch Removal requirement
notes: Requires removal of mercury switches from end-of-life vehicles before delivery to scrap recyclers, effective Sept 2008.

title: Mercury Switch Removal Program (G.S. 130A-310.50)
notes: Statute requiring mercury switch removal from end-of-life vehicles before scrap recycling.

title: An Act To Support and Improve Municipal Recycling Programs and Save Taxpayer Money
notes: Nation's first packaging EPR law (Public Law Ch. 455); stewardship program administered via a PRO.

title: Plastic Pollution and Recycling Modernization Act
notes: Chapter 681 (2021); producer responsibility programs for packaging, printed paper and food serviceware.

title: Producer Responsibility Program for Statewide Recycling
notes: Chapter 337 (2022); producers of packaging and paper fund a statewide recycling system through a PRO.

title: Plastic Pollution Prevention and Packaging Producer Responsibility Act
notes: Chapter 75 (2022); largest U.S. packaging EPR program with source-reduction and recyclability targets by 2032.

title: Packaging Waste and Cost Reduction Act
notes: Chapter 116 (2024); fifth state to enact packaging/paper EPR, within the environment omnibus.

title: Environment - Packaging and Paper Products - Producer Responsibility Plans
notes: Chapter 431 (2025); sixth state to enact packaging/paper EPR, with ecomodulation provisions.

title: Recycling Reform Act
notes: Chapter 316, Laws of 2025; seventh state to enact packaging/paper EPR.

title: An Act Concerning Extended Producer Responsibility for Tires
notes: Public Act 23-62; first-in-the-nation tire EPR program requiring manufacturers to finance post-consumer tire management.

title: California Tire Recycling Act
notes: Fee-funded statewide waste tire recycling and stewardship program administered by CalRecycle.

title: Solid Waste Policy and Management Act - Waste Tires (2025 amendments)
notes: Act 33 of 2025; modernizes the fee-funded waste tire program (SC Code 44-96-170).

title: Vehicle Tire Storage and Recycling - Tire Deposit System (RIGL 23-63-4.9)
notes: P.L. 1992 Ch. 133; refundable per-tire deposit and retailer take-back/disposal responsibility.

title: Pesticide Container Recycling (Food & Agricultural Code 12841.4)
notes: Requires pesticide registrants selling in rigid HDPE containers to establish or participate in a container recycling program.

title: Pesticide Product Disposal and Container Recycling
notes: Chapter 155 (2026); creates a Pesticide Product Disposal and Container Recycling Enterprise.

title: Unused Prescription Drug, Needle, and Syringe Disposal Program (18 V.S.A. 4224)
notes: Producer-financed drug take-back program codified at 18 V.S.A. 4224, administered by the Department of Health.

title: Drug Stewardship Program (M.G.L. c.94G)
notes: Acts of 2016, Chapter 52; manufacturers of covered drugs must operate or fund an approved take-back program.

title: Solid waste: pharmaceutical and sharps waste stewardship
notes: Producer-funded stewardship program for covered drugs administered by CalRecycle; also covers home-generated sharps.

title: New York State Drug Take-Back Act (Public Health Law 290-294)
notes: Chapter 120 of the Laws of 2018; drug manufacturers must establish, fund, and manage a statewide take-back program.

title: Secure Drug Take-Back Act (Chapter 69.48 RCW)
notes: First state law requiring covered manufacturers to pay all costs of a statewide drug take-back program.

title: Safe Drug Disposal / Drug Take-Back Program
notes: Oregon Laws 2019 ch. 659; manufacturer-funded statewide drug take-back stewardship program.

title: Drug take-back stewardship program (38 M.R.S. 1612)
notes: PL 2021 ch. 94; manufacturers must pay all costs of a stewardship program for household pharmaceutical waste.

title: Drug Take-Back Act (410 ILCS 720)
notes: Public Act 102-1055; pharmaceutical manufacturers must establish and finance a drug take-back program.

title: Solid waste: pharmaceutical and sharps waste stewardship
notes: Only U.S. state EPR law for home-generated medical sharps; same statute as the CA pharma program.

title: Household Hazardous Waste Extended Producer Responsibility law (Act 58)
notes: First-in-nation HHW EPR law (Act 58); producers of covered household hazardous products must form a stewardship organization.

title: An Act Concerning Extended Producer Responsibility for Gas Cylinders
notes: Public Act 22-27; first-in-nation EPR law for consumer gas cylinders (CGS 22a-905h).
